classObjective: Solve absolute value inequalities and rewrite them as compound inequalities using and or or.
workedExamples: 6-4, Example 1 — Solve Less-Than Absolute Value Inequalities (|X| < a)
6-4, Example 2 — Solve Greater-Than Absolute Value Inequalities (|X| > a)
6-4, Example 3 — Real-World Applications of Absolute Value Inequalities
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: |X| < a becomes -a < X < a; |X| > a becomes X < -a or X > a; compound inequality conversion
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice solving absolute value inequalities and converting to compound forms
independentPractice: Practice problems solving absolute value inequalities using the compound inequality method
assessment: Exit ticket: solve |2x - 3| < 7 and express as a compound inequality
capReflection: Why does |X| < a become a compound inequality while |X| > a becomes an 'or' inequality?
